Give me: a compelling title, a short intro explaining the value and how to use it, the checklist items in the correct logical or chronological order, a brief note under any item that needs explanation, and a closing section pointing to my offer [YOUR OFFER] as the next step. Make it genuinely complete and usable on its own so it builds trust. Keep it focused enough to deliver a real quick win without overwhelming. My tone: [TONE].
description: Build a complete, usable checklist lead magnet that delivers a quick win.
useCase: Use this to create a checklist opt in fast. Checklists are among the highest converting lead magnets because they promise a clear, achievable outcome, and one that genuinely helps builds the trust that makes the next step toward your offer feel natural.

title: Build a Template or Swipe File Lead Magnet
promptText: Build a template or swipe file lead magnet for [TARGET AUDIENCE] who need help with [TASK OR DELIVERABLE]. Give me: a title, a short how to use it intro, the actual template or set of swipeable examples with clear fill in the blank placeholders where they customize, guidance notes so they use it correctly, and a closing that positions my offer [YOUR OFFER] as the deeper help. Make the template immediately usable and genuinely time saving, since a template that works builds instant credibility. My topic area: [YOUR TOPIC], my tone: [TONE].
description: Build a ready to use template or swipe file that saves your audience real time.
useCase: Use this to create a template opt in, one of the stickiest lead magnet types. A template that saves real work delivers instant value and gets used repeatedly, keeping your brand present and building the credibility that makes people trust your paid offer.

title: Outline an Ebook or Guide
promptText: Outline an ebook or comprehensive guide titled around helping [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [MAIN OUTCOME]. My expertise: [YOUR EXPERTISE], my offer it should lead toward: [YOUR OFFER]. Give me: a working title and subtitle, an introduction that frames the problem and promises the transformation, the chapters in a logical progression that takes the reader from where they are to the outcome, a note on what each chapter covers, and a conclusion that transitions to my offer. Structure it to be genuinely valuable and complete, positioning me as the expert. Note the ideal length per chapter. My tone: [TONE].
description: Outline a valuable ebook or guide that positions you as the expert.
useCase: Use this to plan a substantial lead magnet or authority building ebook. A well structured guide that delivers a real transformation establishes your expertise and captures higher intent leads, since people who download and read a full guide are more invested than casual opt ins.

title: Write an Ebook Chapter
promptText: Write a chapter for my ebook aimed at [TARGET AUDIENCE]. The chapter topic is [CHAPTER TOPIC] and its goal is to help the reader [CHAPTER GOAL]. Here are the key points to cover: [KEY POINTS]. Write it to teach clearly with examples, keep the reader engaged with a mix of explanation and application, include any relevant framework or step by step, and end with a transition to the next chapter. Match my voice using these notes on my style: [YOUR STYLE OR PASTE A SAMPLE]. Keep it valuable and readable, not padded to seem substantial. Target length: [TARGET LENGTH].
description: Write a clear, engaging ebook chapter that teaches and flows to the next.
useCase: Use this to draft ebook chapters efficiently after outlining. Writing chapter by chapter with your key points and voice keeps a long guide consistent and valuable, turning the daunting task of writing an ebook into manageable focused sections.
variables: YOUR STYLE OR PASTE A SAMPLE

title: Write Lead Magnet Landing Page Copy
promptText: Write landing page copy for my lead magnet [LEAD MAGNET NAME], a [FORMAT] that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [OUTCOME]. Give me: a headline that leads with the outcome, a subheadline that adds specificity, 3 to 5 bullet points on what they get and the benefit of each, a short trust or credibility line, and a clear call to action for the opt in. Sell the transformation, not the format, and keep it focused since a lead magnet page should convert fast with minimal friction. Give me 2 headline options to test. My tone: [TONE].
description: Write high converting opt in page copy that sells the lead magnet's outcome.
useCase: Use this for the landing page where visitors decide to give their email. Copy that sells the transformation rather than the format, with clear benefit bullets and low friction, is what converts traffic into subscribers, the entire point of a lead magnet.

title: Generate Lead Magnet Titles
promptText: Generate 15 title options for my lead magnet, a [FORMAT] that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [OUTCOME OR SOLVE PROBLEM]. Give me a mix: some that lead with the specific result, some with a timeframe or number, some that name the pain it removes, and some with a curiosity angle. The title should make the value instantly clear and irresistible enough to trade an email for. Avoid vague or generic titles. Mark the 3 strongest and explain what makes each compelling. My tone: [TONE], my topic: [YOUR TOPIC].
description: Generate irresistible lead magnet titles that make the value instantly clear.
useCase: Use this because the title largely determines your opt in rate. A title that makes the value instantly clear and feels worth an email address converts browsers into subscribers, while a vague one lets even a great lead magnet go undownloaded.

title: Write an Ebook Title and Subtitle
promptText: Write title and subtitle options for my ebook that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [MAIN OUTCOME] and covers [WHAT IT COVERS]. Give me 8 title and subtitle pairs where the title is memorable and intriguing and the subtitle clarifies the specific promise and audience. Vary the approaches: some benefit driven, some intriguing or metaphorical with a clear subtitle, some straightforward and authoritative. The pair together should signal both appeal and substance. Mark the strongest 3 and explain why. My tone: [TONE].
description: Write ebook title and subtitle pairs that balance intrigue with a clear promise.
useCase: Use this to name an ebook or major guide. A strong title and subtitle pair signals both appeal and substance, which drives downloads and shares, and getting the naming right frames how readers perceive the value of everything inside.

title: Write an Ebook Introduction
promptText: Write the introduction for my ebook that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [MAIN OUTCOME]. Give me an intro that hooks the reader immediately, shows I understand their problem and current frustration, promises the transformation the book delivers, briefly establishes why I am qualified to guide them using [YOUR CREDIBILITY], sets expectations for what is ahead, and motivates them to keep reading. Keep it warm and compelling, not a dry preface. The intro should make readers feel they found the right resource. Target length: [TARGET LENGTH], my tone: [TONE].
description: Write an ebook introduction that hooks readers and earns their commitment.
useCase: Use this to write the intro that determines whether people read your ebook. A strong introduction that shows understanding, promises transformation, and establishes credibility earns the reader's commitment, since a guide that gets read builds far more trust than one that gets downloaded and abandoned.

title: Build a Resource List Lead Magnet
promptText: Build a curated resource list lead magnet for [TARGET AUDIENCE] who want to [GOAL]. Give me: a title, a short intro on why this curated list saves them time and confusion, the resources or tools organized into logical categories, a one or two line note on each explaining what it is and when to use it, and a closing pointing to my offer [YOUR OFFER]. My commentary and curation are the value, so make the notes genuinely useful. Here are resources to include or the categories to cover: [RESOURCES OR CATEGORIES]. My tone: [TONE].
description: Build a curated resource list where your commentary is the real value.
useCase: Use this to create a resource list opt in that positions you as a trusted filter. Curation with genuine commentary saves your audience the work of finding and vetting tools themselves, and being the source of the definitive list in your niche is a durable authority position.

title: Design an Email Mini Course Lead Magnet
promptText: Design a free email mini course that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] achieve [OUTCOME] over [NUMBER OF DAYS OR LESSONS] emails, leading toward my offer [YOUR OFFER]. Give me: a course title and promise, the learning arc across the lessons so each builds on the last, the specific topic and takeaway for each lesson email, and where and how to introduce my offer naturally near the end. Each lesson should deliver one clear win so subscribers stay engaged through the sequence. Note the subject line for each email. My tone: [TONE].
description: Design a multi day email mini course that teaches and warms leads toward your offer.
useCase: Use this to create a mini course lead magnet, which builds deeper engagement than a one time download. A multi day course trains subscribers to open your emails and delivers repeated wins, warming them toward your offer far more effectively than a single freebie.

title: Write Opt In Form Copy
promptText: Write opt in form copy for my lead magnet [LEAD MAGNET NAME] that helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] with [OUTCOME]. This is the compact form itself, such as a popup, inline box, or sidebar widget. Give me: a short punchy headline, one line of supporting text on the benefit, the button text that beats the generic submit, and a reassurance line about no spam or easy unsubscribe. Keep it tight since form space is limited and every word must earn its place. Give me 3 versions for different placements: popup, inline, and footer. My tone: [TONE].
description: Write tight opt in form copy that converts across popups, inline, and footer.
useCase: Use this for the actual opt in forms across your site. Compact form copy with a benefit driven headline and a button that beats generic submit text lifts conversion at the exact moment of decision, and different placements need different tuned versions.

title: Write the Lead Magnet Delivery Email
promptText: Write the delivery email that sends [LEAD MAGNET NAME] to new subscribers who just opted in. Give me: a subject line confirming their download is inside, a warm welcome, the clear link or access to the lead magnet, a quick tip on how to get the most from it, a taste of what else they can expect from me, and a soft first mention of my offer [YOUR OFFER] without pushing. This email has a high open rate so it is prime real estate to start the relationship well. Keep it warm and concise. My tone: [TONE].
description: Write the high open rate delivery email that starts the subscriber relationship right.
useCase: Use this for the email that delivers your lead magnet, which gets opened almost every time. This prime real estate is where you set expectations and begin the relationship, and a warm delivery email that adds value beyond the download starts subscribers on the path toward your offer.

title: Build a Quiz Lead Magnet
promptText: Design a quiz lead magnet for [TARGET AUDIENCE] that helps them discover [WHAT THE QUIZ REVEALS, E.G. THEIR TYPE, THEIR BIGGEST GAP, OR WHICH APPROACH FITS]. It should lead toward my offer [YOUR OFFER]. Give me: a compelling quiz title, the questions that segment respondents meaningfully, the result categories or types, a description of what each result means with tailored advice, and how each result naturally points to a relevant next step or my offer. The quiz should be genuinely insightful and shareable, and the results should feel personalized. My topic: [YOUR TOPIC], my tone: [TONE].
description: Build an insightful, shareable quiz that segments leads and points to your offer.
useCase: Use this to create a quiz lead magnet, which converts and shares exceptionally well. Quizzes engage through curiosity about the personalized result, and segmenting subscribers by their answers lets you tailor follow up and recommend the right offer to each type.
variables: WHAT THE QUIZ REVEALS, E.G. THEIR TYPE, THEIR BIGGEST GAP, OR WHICH APPROACH FITS

title: Build a Cheat Sheet Lead Magnet
promptText: Build a one page cheat sheet lead magnet for [TARGET AUDIENCE] that distills [TOPIC OR PROCESS] into a quick reference they will keep and use repeatedly. Give me: a title, the key information organized for fast scanning such as steps, formulas, do and do not lists, or quick reference tables, and a note on layout so it fits usefully on one page. Include the most important things they need at a glance without hunting. End with a small pointer to my offer [YOUR OFFER]. A great cheat sheet earns a permanent spot on their desk or screen. My tone: [TONE].
description: Build a one page cheat sheet people keep and reference again and again.
useCase: Use this to create a cheat sheet lead magnet, valued for its repeated usefulness. A quick reference people keep on their desk or screen keeps your brand present over time, and the perceived high value relative to the small effort of downloading drives strong opt in rates.

title: Turn Existing Content into a Lead Magnet
promptText: Here is existing content I have created: [PASTE BLOG POSTS, VIDEOS, OR MATERIAL, OR DESCRIBE THEM]. Help me repackage it into a lead magnet for [TARGET AUDIENCE]. Identify the strongest reusable material, recommend the best lead magnet format to turn it into, and outline how to restructure and upgrade it into a cohesive downloadable resource that feels more valuable than the free scattered version. It should lead toward my offer [YOUR OFFER]. Note what to add to make it worth opting in for rather than just a repackage. My tone: [TONE].
description: Repackage your existing content into a cohesive, opt in worthy lead magnet.
useCase: Use this to create a lead magnet from work you already did. Repurposing your best existing content into a cohesive downloadable resource is far faster than starting fresh, and packaging scattered free material into one valuable asset gives people a reason to opt in.
variables: PASTE BLOG POSTS, VIDEOS, OR MATERIAL, OR DESCRIBE THEM

title: Build a Workbook or Worksheet Lead Magnet
promptText: Build a workbook or worksheet lead magnet that guides [TARGET AUDIENCE] through [PROCESS OR EXERCISE] to reach [OUTCOME]. Give me: a title, a short intro on what they will accomplish, the exercises or prompts in sequence with space for them to fill in their own answers, guiding questions that produce real insight or a tangible result by the end, and a closing that connects their result to my offer [YOUR OFFER]. Make it interactive so they invest effort, which increases commitment and results. Note where an example answer would help. My topic: [YOUR TOPIC], my tone: [TONE].
description: Build an interactive workbook that guides your audience to a real result.
useCase: Use this to create a workbook lead magnet, which drives high engagement through active participation. When subscribers fill in exercises and produce a tangible result, they invest effort that deepens commitment, and reaching a real outcome makes your paid offer the obvious next step.

title: Bridge Your Lead Magnet to Your Offer
promptText: My lead magnet is [LEAD MAGNET NAME], which helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] with [WHAT IT SOLVES]. My paid offer is [YOUR OFFER], which delivers [OFFER OUTCOME]. Help me design the bridge between them so the lead magnet naturally leads to the offer. Show me: the logical connection where the lead magnet solves the first problem and the offer solves the next, how to end the lead magnet so it opens the door to the offer without a hard sell, and the follow up messaging that continues the journey. The transition should feel like a natural next step, not a bait and switch. My tone: [TONE].
description: Design the natural bridge from your free lead magnet to your paid offer.
useCase: Use this when your lead magnet gets downloads but does not lead to sales. Designing the bridge so the freebie solves the first problem and your offer solves the next makes the transition feel like a natural next step, which is what turns a list into revenue.

title: Audit an Underperforming Lead Magnet
promptText: My lead magnet [LEAD MAGNET NAME] for [TARGET AUDIENCE] is not converting well. Here is the current title and landing page copy: [PASTE TITLE AND COPY], and here is what it delivers: [WHAT IT DELIVERS]. Audit it to find why it may be underperforming. Assess: whether the promise is clear and compelling enough, whether it targets a real urgent problem, whether the title earns the opt in, whether the landing copy sells the transformation, and whether it attracts the right people who would buy my offer [YOUR OFFER]. Then give me specific fixes prioritized by likely impact. Be direct about weaknesses.
description: Audit a low converting lead magnet and get prioritized fixes.
useCase: Use this when a lead magnet gets traffic but few opt ins, or attracts the wrong people. An honest audit of the promise, title, copy, and targeting reveals why it underperforms, so you fix the specific weak points rather than scrapping it or guessing at improvements.

title: Build a Case Study Lead Magnet
promptText: Build a case study lead magnet showing how [CLIENT OR EXAMPLE] achieved [RESULT] using [YOUR APPROACH OR OFFER], for [TARGET AUDIENCE] considering similar results. Give me: a results focused title, the starting situation and challenge, the approach and steps taken presented so readers can learn from it, the results with specifics, and the transferable lessons. Structure it to teach and prove at once, so it both delivers value and demonstrates my offer works. End with a natural invitation to explore working with me. The reader should think this could work for me too. My tone: [TONE].
description: Build a case study lead magnet that teaches while proving your offer works.
useCase: Use this to create a lead magnet that doubles as powerful proof. A case study attracts leads who want the same result and simultaneously demonstrates your offer works, capturing high intent prospects who are already imagining themselves achieving the outcome you show.

title: Create a Content Upgrade for a Blog Post
promptText: I have a blog post about [BLOG POST TOPIC] for [TARGET AUDIENCE]. Design a content upgrade, a bonus resource offered within the post in exchange for an email, that perfectly complements it. Given the post covers [WHAT THE POST COVERS], suggest the ideal upgrade format and content that extends the post's value, such as a checklist, template, expanded guide, or worksheet version of the post's advice. Give me the upgrade concept, its title, an outline of what it contains, and the in post copy to offer it. A tightly matched content upgrade converts far better than a generic sitewide lead magnet. My tone: [TONE].
description: Design a tightly matched content upgrade that boosts opt ins within a blog post.
useCase: Use this to dramatically lift opt ins on your best blog posts. A content upgrade matched to a specific post converts far better than a generic sitewide lead magnet, because readers already engaged with the topic want the bonus that extends exactly what they are reading.

title: Write a Design Brief for Your Ebook
promptText: I have written my ebook [EBOOK NAME] for [TARGET AUDIENCE] and need it to look professional. Write a design brief I can hand to a designer or use myself in a tool like Canva. Based on the content covering [WHAT IT COVERS] and my brand feel of [BRAND STYLE OR VIBE], recommend: the overall visual direction, cover concept, layout structure for readability, where to place visuals or callouts, and any elements that increase perceived value. The goal is a polished, credible looking resource that matches the quality of the content. Keep the guidance practical for a non designer. My tone: [TONE].
description: Write a practical design brief that makes your ebook look professional.
useCase: Use this once your ebook content is written but looks plain. A clear design brief turns good content into a polished, credible resource, since a professional looking lead magnet raises perceived value and trust, whether you hand the brief to a designer or execute it yourself in Canva.

title: Plan How to Promote Your Lead Magnet
promptText: I created a lead magnet [LEAD MAGNET NAME] for [TARGET AUDIENCE] but need to get it in front of people. My channels are [YOUR CHANNELS, E.G. INSTAGRAM, EMAIL, BLOG, LINKEDIN]. Give me a promotion plan: where and how to place it for ongoing passive opt ins across my site and profiles, the social posts and hooks to actively promote it, how to work it into my content naturally, and any partnership or one time push ideas. Prioritize the highest leverage promotion for a solo operator with limited time. A great lead magnet nobody sees builds no list. My tone: [TONE].
description: Build a practical promotion plan so your lead magnet actually gets seen.
useCase: Use this after creating a lead magnet, when the real work of getting it seen begins. A promotion plan covering both passive placements and active pushes ensures your lead magnet actually builds a list, since even the best magnet grows nothing if nobody finds it.
variables: YOUR CHANNELS, E.G. INSTAGRAM, EMAIL, BLOG, LINKEDIN
